threats.title: Incompatible IERC20 Interface Causes Permanent DoS for Non-Standard Stablecoins
threats.hypothesis_description: The contract uses the standard IERC20 interface which expects a boolean return value. Non-standard stablecoins like USDT do not return a boolean on transfer/transferFrom. The EVM will revert these calls, rendering the AMM completely unusable for the most popular USD stablecoin.
threats.investigation_rationale: Since the protocol is explicitly designed for 'USD stablecoins', the inability to support USDT represents a critical failure of the core business logic and prevents protocol adoption.
deployment_assumptions: Pool is created using USDT or other non-standard ERC20 tokens that do not return a boolean
threats.config_evidence: FeeAMM.sol imports standard OpenZeppelin IERC20.sol and does not use SafeERC20.
preconditions: Target token does not return a boolean on transfer/transferFrom (e.g., USDT on mainnet)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Standard interface expects 32 bytes of return data, EVM reverts if 0 bytes returned
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Any interaction with the pool will revert, locking any accidentally sent funds or preventing usage
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, but causes complete denial of service and potential loss of utility

economic_questions: Which specific target chains and stablecoins are intended for deployment?
Are there any wrapper contracts planned to handle non-standard tokens?
validation_requirements: Test contract interactions using a mainnet fork with actual USDT contract
Verify EVM behavior on the specific target chain

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Prevents market making for major stablecoins

threats.title: Missing Slippage Protection Exposes LPs to Severe Composition Risk
threats.hypothesis_description: The burn function lacks minimum output parameters. If the pool's composition changes significantly between transaction submission and execution, the LP will receive an unexpected and potentially unfavorable ratio of tokens.
threats.investigation_rationale: LPs expect a certain ratio of tokens when withdrawing. Without slippage bounds, normal market activity (like a large rebalanceSwap) can drastically alter the payout, causing economic harm to the LP.
threats.config_evidence: No deployment evidence required; inherent to contract logic.

proof_of_signal.pattern: burn signature lacks minAmountUserToken and minAmountValidatorToken parameters

preconditions: Another user executes a large swap before the burn executes

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: reserveUserToken and reserveValidatorToken can be heavily skewed by preceding transactions
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attackers can intentionally skew the pool to force LPs to absorb unwanted inventory

economic_questions: What is the maximum composition shift possible in a single block?
How much value can an LP lose due to receiving the less desirable token?
validation_requirements: Simulate a large rebalanceSwap immediately followed by a burn
Calculate the value difference of the received tokens vs expected tokens

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Sufficient capital to significantly move pool reserves
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Depends on the external market value difference between the two stablecoins
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Low, primarily causes loss to victim rather than direct profit

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Large liquidity relative to pool size
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When an LP submits a large burn transaction
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Forces LPs to bear inventory risk without consent

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Receiving 100% of one token instead of a 50/50 split
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: The LP's entire withdrawn liquidity is subject to composition risk

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Pool reserve ratios before and after large swaps
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Value of withdrawn tokens under extreme pool skews

threats.title: Unchecked Return Values Enable Free Token Extraction
threats.hypothesis_description: The contract does not verify the boolean return values of transfer and transferFrom. For tokens that return false instead of reverting on failure (e.g., ZRX), an attacker can execute swaps without providing input tokens, completely draining the paired reserves.
threats.investigation_rationale: This allows an attacker to artificially inflate pool reserves and extract real tokens without spending any capital, leading to a complete loss of funds for LPs.
deployment_assumptions: Pool is created with at least one token that returns false on failure (e.g., ZRX)
threats.config_evidence: FeeAMM.sol uses standard IERC20 without checking returns or using SafeERC20.
preconditions: Pool contains a token that returns false on failure
Attacker calls swap with 0 allowance for that token

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: State updates proceed even if transferFrom fails silently
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker receives amountOut of the paired token while providing 0 amountIn
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker captures 100% of the amountOut as pure profit

economic_questions: Are there any planned target tokens that exhibit this behavior?
Can an attacker permissionlessly create a pool with such a token to trap users?
validation_requirements: Deploy a mock token that returns false on failure
Execute a swap with 0 allowance and verify extraction of the paired token

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Entire reserve of the paired legitimate token

threats.title: Fixed-Rate Pricing Model Guarantees LP Depletion During Market Depegs
threats.hypothesis_description: The AMM uses fixed multipliers (M=9970, N=9985) without a bonding curve. If the market price of the paired stablecoins diverges beyond the 0.3% fee spread, arbitrageurs can completely drain the more valuable token at a fixed discount, leaving LPs with 100% of the depegged asset.
threats.investigation_rationale: Stablecoins frequently experience minor depegs. A fixed-rate AMM cannot adjust its price to reflect market realities, making it a guaranteed target for arbitrageurs who will extract all value from LPs.
threats.config_evidence: Constants M and N enforce a fixed exchange rate regardless of pool reserves.
preconditions: Market price of the two stablecoins diverges by more than 0.3%
Arbitrageurs have sufficient capital to execute swaps

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Price is always 0.997 regardless of reserve ratios
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Arbitrageurs buy the depegged asset cheaply on external markets and swap it for the fully pegged asset in the AMM
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Profit = (Market Price Difference - 0.3% fee) * Swap Amount

economic_questions: What is the historical frequency of >0.3% depegs for the target stablecoins?
Is there any off-chain mechanism to pause the contract during a depeg?
validation_requirements: Model LP losses during a simulated 5% depeg event
Calculate the exact arbitrage profit margin

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Sufficient capital to drain the pool (can use flash loans)
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Proportional to the severity of the depeg and pool size

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: External market liquidity to acquire the depegged asset
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: During market volatility or stablecoin depeg events
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Pool becomes 100% composed of the less valuable asset

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Loss of value proportional to the depeg percentage
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: The entire value difference between the pegged and depegged assets in the pool

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Fee spread vs historical stablecoin volatility

threats.title: Fee-on-Transfer Tokens Cause Permanent Pool Insolvency
threats.hypothesis_description: The contract updates internal reserves using the nominal amountIn rather than measuring the actual transferred balance. If a fee-on-transfer token is used, the tracked reserves will exceed the contract's actual balance, eventually causing legitimate LP withdrawals to revert due to insufficient funds.
threats.investigation_rationale: Internal accounting must perfectly match actual contract balances. Divergence leads to insolvency, trapping user funds permanently.
deployment_assumptions: Pool is created with a fee-on-transfer token
threats.config_evidence: Reserves are updated using the function parameter amountIn instead of balance before/after checks.
preconditions: Token applies a fee on transfer
Users perform swaps or mints with this token

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: reserveUserToken increases by amountIn, but actual balance increases by amountIn * (1 - fee)
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Early withdrawers get their full share, leaving late withdrawers with nothing (bank run)

economic_questions: Are fee-on-transfer tokens expected to be supported?
How quickly does the insolvency reach a critical threshold where burns fail?
validation_requirements: Perform several swaps and attempt a full pool burn to verify the revert

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: No direct profit, but early withdrawers steal from late withdrawers

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Up to 100% loss of deposited funds if they cannot withdraw
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All funds in the affected pool

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Divergence rate between reserves and actual balances

threats.title: Zero-Value Transfer Reverts Cause Temporary LP Lockup
threats.hypothesis_description: If a pool's reserveUserToken is completely drained (or is initially 0), burn will calculate amountUserToken = 0. Some ERC20 tokens revert on zero-value transfers. If the pool contains such a token, LPs will be unable to withdraw their validatorToken liquidity until new userToken is swapped into the pool.
threats.investigation_rationale: LPs must be able to withdraw their liquidity at any time. A dependency on the pool having non-zero reserves of both tokens to function creates a brittle withdrawal process.
deployment_assumptions: Pool uses a token that reverts on 0-value transfers
threats.config_evidence: burn function does not check if amountUserToken > 0 before calling transfer.

proof_of_signal.pattern: IERC20(userToken).transfer(to, amountUserToken); where amountUserToken can be 0

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: An attacker could intentionally drain reserveUserToken to DoS withdrawals
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, purely griefing or forcing LPs to wait

economic_questions: Which target tokens revert on 0-value transfers?
How much capital is required to completely drain reserveUserToken?
validation_requirements: Test burn function with a mock token that reverts on 0-value transfers when reserves are 0

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Capital to drain reserveUserToken via rebalanceSwap

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When LPs are trying to exit the pool
